title: Key Point
text: How does $$b$$ contribute to the logarithmic function's properties? Is it an x-intercept?

title: Key Point
text: When a logarithmic function's base and the argument is equal, as $$b$$, what does the value become?

title: Key Point
text: The value becomes 1! So, the graph passes through (b,1), instead of (b,0)
